violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. -Employee returned from bar area and donned gloves to prepare food without hand wash.
Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Sani Bucket was not made/available in kitchen.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Make table found with a temp range of 42 to 50F at top of unit. *Turned unit down from 2 to 3. May need to have unit serviced if inconsistent at holding food temperatures below 41F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Ranch pitcher and portion cups were not date marked for make date. -Sliced turkey and sliced ham in white upright refrigerator were not date marked.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Nacho cheese dated 12/22/25 in 2 door stainless refrigerator. *Removed for disposal.

Temperature measuring devices. — --Temperature measuring device was not accurate or not easily readable. -Thermometer in refrigerator showed 30F and foods found at 40F.--Temperature measuring device was not provided. -Could not locate a thermometer in make table.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Residue at underside of handles of 2 door stainless refrigerator. -Grease/residue at side surfaces of cook line equipment. -Food spill on foil in bottom of right oven. -Carbon build up in bottom of left oven. -Some food spill/residue at interior bottom a make table, white upright refrigerator and in basement pizza freezer. -Limescale/residue at bar mat used for ice scoops and bucket.
